item.title: The American Family Is at a Breaking Point. Our Politics Have Finally Noticed.
item.description: Society has treated parenting as a private endeavor. But what if raising children is also a public good?

item.title: Why It Cost This Man $250,000 to Help His Family Survive in Gaza
item.description: How Israeli trade restrictions and shadowy profiteers drove up the price of nearly everything — from food to evacuation.

item.title: Who Will Actually Thrive in the Hybrid A.I.-Human Work Force
item.description: A panel of experts explains how job seekers should prepare for the future of work.

item.title: How Talking to the ‘Chat’ Crossed Over Into Real Life
item.description: It’s a word ripped from Twitch streams, but it functions as a Greek chorus for an online, isolated generation.

item.title: 6 Takeaways From the Story of How the Epstein Files Paralyzed the White House
item.description: Senior officials clashed in a series of meetings as they struggled to manage a crisis over the president’s refusal to release the documents.

item.title: My Father Abused My Sister When We Were Kids. Should I Tell My Half Siblings?
item.description: I want them to grasp the differences between the two families he created.

item.title: It’s Not Just Food. Our Entire World is ‘Ultraprocessed.’
item.description: The prefix has been used as a marketing tactic for decades. But when life feels extreme, it more often means something negative.

item.title: AIPAC Wants Democrats to Back Israel. Instead, They’re Turning on AIPAC.
item.description: Once the guardian of the bipartisan pro-Israel consensus, it is now a polarizing force in the party.

item.title: ‘Almost There, Almost There, Almost There’: The Hopes and the Heartbreak of the '90s Knicks
item.description: 28 years ago, the team’s nemesis Reggie Miller sunk a shot that dashed their playoff dreams. Here’s what Spike Lee and others sitting courtside remember.

item.title: Can I Break My Husband’s Restaurant Boycott?
item.description: A dispute over how to handle a cracked soup container.

item.title: The Pain of Caring for a Parent Who Abused You
item.description: The United States is reliant on unpaid family caregivers, and millions of adult children are caring for parents who didn’t really care for them.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein’s Jailhouse Writings
item.description: The Times obtained handwritten notes, never before made public, that reveal his state of mind — including signs of suicidal thoughts — before his death.

item.title: Six Takeaways From the Times Investigation Into Epstein’s Death
item.description: We considered every plausible theory of his death, both official and otherwise, seeking out the most persuasive arguments and evidence for each.

item.title: The Untold Story of Jeffrey Epstein’s Death
item.description: New evidence, new interviews and the fullest investigation yet into the question of whether he killed himself.
